reviews.review_description:
Got the game free and uninstalled it within the first   One minute of game play.  The main menu screen had me with its population one type arms and looked promising until the actual game play started.  

It feels like a game that was put together with no direction during development. You are a player standing on a platform that’s in space as asteroids  are flying past you with robots that come from behind said asteroids your suppose to shoot with pistols.  That’s the game. 

1 stars because the main menu screen rocks. 
1 star because it looks ok.

developer_response.body:
Sorry about that, Wareik — we’re aware of the issue with the main menu freezing and are currently working on a patch.
Your feedback helps us locate and fix such issues faster. Please check for updates soon!

reviews.review_title: Pretty fun but initial launch has issue
reviews.review_description: I'm writing this review primarily because my experience has been much better than the handful of other reviews currently posted. It's true, the first time I launched this title it didn't start. Perhaps that's because it needed to cache files, but after waiting a few minutes I just closed it and restarted and it launched fine. Although I don't yet have hours in the game, the time I have spent has been really fun. It's an on-the-rails arcade style shooter. The graphics are good for a Quest title (I've played on a Q3). The main menu adequately explained the controls and after checking that I jumped into a game without difficulty. I haven't yet checked out multi-player, but the single-player campaign is fun and gameplay is intuitive. The dev obviously put time and effort and since I've been enjoying it (and will continue to play) I felt compelled to post a more favorable review. The dev made the game free for a short time prior to the official launch and the negative reviews probably reflect some issues from that earlier version. The game could still use some updates but it is fun, looks good, and deserves more consideration than those earlier reviews indicate. I'm in no way associated with the dev--I just found it to be pretty fun and after reading some negative reviews wanted to let folks know it's a really decent little game. If the dev chooses to do updates and fixes whatever causes it to not launch properly after installing, it'll be a GREAT little game.

developer_response.body:
Thanks, TheForbin! We’re aware of the launch issue and are currently working on a fix. We really appreciate you taking the time to report it.
We’re improving ROBOCRACY step by step — your feedback helps us move faster!

reviews.review_description: About as 1-dimensionsl as you can get.

developer_response.body:
We appreciate your honesty, QUACKBIRD. ROBOCRACY is still being developed and improved — new gameplay features and missions are on the way.
We’d love to hear more detailed feedback about what you’d like to see improved.

reviews.review_description: Will try when I get home

developer_response.body:
Hi RoJoBo! We noticed that your previous comment might not have appeared on Meta. We’d really appreciate it if you could share your thoughts again after trying ROBOCRACY.
Player feedback helps us a lot in improving the game and fixing bugs — every review matters!
